title: Mirror-symmetric geometry: descriptor tie must yield NeedsRepair, never a guess
source: OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_elementmap_rigorous.cpp:309-335 — testFallbackRefusesSymmetricTwins: a stale face centered between two equidistant same-kind twins; resolveWithFallback MUST return a null shape (refuse) rather than pick one. assert(rematched.IsNull()).

OneCAD-CPP/src/kernel/elementmap/ElementMap.h:256-261 — the uniqueness-margin guard: with two near-equidistant candidates 'a re-match is a coin flip — refuse rather than guess' (secondBest - best < max(0.5, best) => return null).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_elementmap_rigorous.cpp:282-307 — testRebindRejectsFarMatches: no close candidate => entry LOSES its shape (loud clearShape) instead of migrating to a wrong face. assert(farEntry->shape.IsNull()).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_elementmap_rigorous.cpp:221-257 — testResolveWithFallbackRematchesAndRejects: literal hit score 0; stale id re-matches a near-identical same-owner face (score<5); rejects when no same-kind candidate exists.
OneCAD-CPP/TODO.md:149 — H5-A shipped: 'resolveWithFallback 5.0->1.0 + uniqueness margin; global best-first assignment with deterministic tie-breaks'.
protocol/SCHEMA.md §9 (NeedsRepair candidates, margin), §10 (auto-bind iff score1>=0.85 AND score1-score2>=0.10; symmetric tie => NeedsRepair).

expected-values/proto_elementmap_rigorous NOTE — proto_elementmap_rigorous is a REQUIRED-before-commit test (CLAUDE.md) but was not among the three whose stdout was captured; it is assertion-based (boolean), so its meaning is recorded here rather than a numeric recording.

opScript.description: Construct mirror-symmetric geometry so a reference resolves to TWO equally-good candidates. In the C++ unit the twins are the top faces of a left box [-20..-10] and a right box [10..20], with the stale reference sitting exactly between them (top face of a centered box [-5..5]). In the new stack the analog is a symmetric part where a fillet/host reference could bind to either mirror-image edge/face after an edit.

intent.descriptor: frozen descriptor of the centered/original face

opScript.note: Dry-run ladder execution (SCHEMA §7.5 ResolveRefs). Two candidates score equally.

oldStack.outcome: REFUSE — resolveWithFallback returns a null shape (no bind). The downstream op then fails loudly / the entry loses its shape; it never silently migrates onto one of the twins.
oldStack.meaning: The old kernel already prefers 'loud failure over wrong-face' (H5-A). It has no user-facing repair path — it just refuses and the reference breaks.

newStack_requiredOutcome.outcome: NeedsRepair STATE with both candidates surfaced.

candidates.note: left twin — illustrative scores per SCHEMA §9 example

newStack_requiredOutcome.policy: auto-bind requires score1>=0.85 AND (score1-score2)>=0.10; a 0.91/0.91 tie has margin 0.00 < 0.10 => NeedsRepair. A false positive (wrong silent bind) is strictly worse than a false negative (asking the user). SCHEMA §10.
newStack_requiredOutcome.notAnError: NeedsRepair is per-step STATE inside PlanPrepared, never an error object (SCHEMA §8). Repair = Rust rewrites the OperationRecord ref + re-regen; there is no worker BindRepair verb (SCHEMA §9).
newStack_requiredOutcome.scoresCaveat: 0.91/0.91 are the SCHEMA §9 EXAMPLE values illustrating a tie, NOT captured from OneCAD-CPP (whose score() is an unbounded cost, not a normalized confidence). The load-bearing expectation is the OUTCOME (NeedsRepair on tie), not the specific score.

farMatchControl.scenario: Reference whose geometry is gone and every candidate is far (~60mm) away.
farMatchControl.outcome: entry loses its shape (loud clearShape), does NOT migrate to unrelated geometry.

notes: Companion to case_e. This is the calibration fixture that guarantees the resolver refuses to guess under symmetry. The threshold moved from the old max(0.5, best) cost-margin to the new normalized 0.10 confidence-margin, but the invariant is identical: never silently bind a symmetric tie. All scores here are illustrative; only the refuse/NeedsRepair OUTCOME is asserted by the old stack (as a null-shape refusal).
